channel.title: Indian Defence Research Wing
channel.description: INDIAN DEFENCE NEWS, LATEST DEFENCE NEWS, INDIAN ARMY, INDIAN AIR FORCE, INDIAN NAVY, DEFENSE NEWS

item.title: India’s RDE Cruise Missile Strategy: How Low-Cost Supersonic Swarms Could Protect BrahMos for High-Value Strikes
item.description: India’s emerging work on Rotating Detonation Engine (RDE) propulsion is opening the door to a fundamentally new strike doctrine—one that separates mass from precision and uses economics as a weapon. Saurav Jha, Founder and CEO of D-Propulse, has indicated that RDE-powered cruise missiles could be deployed in large numbers for saturation attacks, allowing premium assets like the BrahMos to be reserved exclusively for high-value targets. This concept, if operationalised, could significantly alter the cost-exchange dynamics of modern air defence warfare.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Sheshnaag-150 Loitering Munition features a High-Performance Boxer Engine for 1,000 km Strike Missions
item.description: The Sheshnaag-150 long-range loitering munition has made its first detailed public appearance, drawing attention not just for its reported 1,000 km range but also for the propulsion system powering it. At the center of interest is a 4-cylinder, horizontally opposed (boxer) air-cooled piston engine, a configuration typically associated with larger tactical UAVs in the Searcher and Heron class.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Indian Army Clears DRDO’s MPATGM for Procurement, Indigenous Anti-Tank Missile to Equip Infantry Units
item.description: In a significant boost to India’s infantry combat capabilities and indigenous defence ecosystem, the Indian Army has cleared the Man Portable Anti-Tank Guided Missile (MPATGM), developed by the Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO), for procurement. The decision marks a critical milestone in replacing or supplementing imported anti-tank systems with a fully indigenous solution tailored for Indian operational conditions.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: HAL Targets AI & AR-Based Visual Inspection System for HTT-40 Engine Maintenance
item.description: Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL) has launched another advanced DRISHTI challenge focused on developing an AI and Augmented Reality (AR)-assisted comprehensive visual inspection system for the TPE331-12B engine powering the HTT-40 trainer aircraft. The initiative is aimed at modernising inspection workflows, reducing manual dependency, and enhancing accuracy and efficiency in engine maintenance processes.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Indian Navy Seeks Virtual Walkthrough Model for Kalvari-Class Submarines to Transform Training
item.description: The Indian Navy is moving to modernize submarine training by seeking the development of an Interactive Virtual Walkthrough Trainer (IVWT) for its Kalvari-class submarines under the DISC 14 innovation challenge. The initiative aims to bridge a critical training gap by replacing traditional, resource-intensive methods with an immersive digital learning environment.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: BEL Targets AI-Based System for Detection and Cyber Takeover of Hostile Drones
item.description: Bharat Electronics Limited (BEL) has launched a new DRISHTI challenge aimed at developing an advanced counter-unmanned aerial system (C-UAS) capable of detecting, classifying, tracking, and executing cyber takeover of hostile drones operating in contested airspace. The initiative reflects India’s growing focus on counter-drone technologies as unmanned aerial threats continue to evolve in both conventional and asymmetric warfare scenarios.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Policy to improve relations with India remains unchanged, says China
item.description: China on Tuesday said its policy to improve relations with India has not changed as it defended its move to publish new names for various regions in Arunachal Pradesh. India on Sunday categorically rejected China’s efforts to assign “fictitious names” to Indian territory, asserting that such attempts to create “baseless narratives” cannot alter “undeniable reality” and could derail efforts to normalise bilateral ties.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Baloch Armed Group Launches Naval Wing, Claims 3 Pakistani Soldiers Killed In First Op
item.description: The Baloch Liberation Army (BLA) claimed that three Pakistani naval personnel were killed in what it described as its first maritime operation in the Jiwani area of Gwadar district in Balochistan, as it announced the formation of a naval wing.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Indian Army and IAF Expand Attack Helicopter Fleets as Prachand Induction Accelerates
item.description: India’s armed forces are steadily expanding their dedicated attack helicopter fleets as part of a broader modernisation drive, with a strong emphasis on enhancing battlefield mobility, precision strike capability, and now even air defence roles against emerging drone threats.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: ISI’s CCTV Spy Web Extended To Toll Plazas, Aimed At Tracking India’s Lifelines
item.description: The ongoing investigations into the ISI’s CCTV camera espionage case have revealed that apart from railway stations and military installations, the accused persons were also told to keep a close watch on toll plazas. This shows the extent of data that the ISI sought to mine across the country.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Dawood-Linked Fake Currency Syndicate Goes Local As Border Routes Tighten
item.description: Indian agencies have reported a sharp rise in counterfeit currency seizures, pointing to a renewed push by organised networks to expand operations. Investigators say the syndicate linked to Dawood Ibrahim, operating from Pakistan, is increasingly directing operatives in India to print fake notes locally instead of relying on cross-border smuggling routes through Pakistan and Bangladesh.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: ‘Ab kya hoga, ye rab jane…’: Tharoor takes dig at failed US-Iran talks in Pakistan
item.description: Senior Congress leader Shashi Tharoor on Monday took a swipe at the “failed” US-Iran peace talks in Pakistan with an Urdu couplet, saying only god knows now what will happen. “Ab kya hoga, ye rab jane; Na woh mane, na ye mane (only god knows what will happen now as both sides did not agree),” Tharoor said on X, tagging a post-talks video clip of US Vice President JD Vance, who led the American delegation at the negotiations in Islamabad.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: EAM Jaishankar And Israeli Counterpart Discuss West Asia Situation, Strait Of Hormuz
item.description: External Affairs Minister (EAM) S Jaishankar held a telephonic conversation with his Israeli counterpart Gideon Sa’ar on Tuesday, discussing West Asia situation and the Strait of Hormuz. “Had a telecon this afternoon with Israel FM Gideon Sa’ar. Our discussion covered different aspects of the West Asia situation,” EAM Jaishankar posted on X. Sa’ar stated that he told EAM Jaishankar that the US’ stance in the negotiations that would prevent Iran from obtaining nuclear weapons is important for the international community.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Pak media watchdog issues notice to channel for running ‘Indian content’ while reporting news of Asha Bhosle’s death
item.description: Pakistan’s media watchdog has issued a show-cause notice to a leading news channel for running “Indian content” while broadcasting the news about the death of legendary singer Asha Bhosle.A ban on Indian content in Pakistan has been in place since 2018.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Top Army officer reviews anti-infiltration grid along LoC in Jammu
item.description: General Officer Commanding of Army’s White Knight Corps Lt Gen P K Mishra on Monday visited Akhnoor sector near Jammu to assess the operational preparedness of the troops guarding the Line of Control (LoC) with a key focus on counter-infiltration measures.Lt Gen Mishra was accompanied by GOC Crossed Swords Division, also known as 10th Infantry division which is a vital formation responsible for securing the LoC in the Akhnoor sector.
item.dc:creator: admin
